flashcards.front: What do vertices represent in exam scheduling using graph coloring?
flashcards.back: Vertices represent different courses or exams that need to be scheduled.

flashcards.front: What is the key constraint in register allocation that creates edges in the interference graph?
flashcards.back: Two variables that are live at the same time cannot share the same register, creating an edge between them.

flashcards.front: What does the Four Color Theorem state about map coloring?
flashcards.back: Any planar map can be colored with at most four colors such that no two adjacent regions have the same color.

flashcards.front: In frequency assignment, what do the colors represent?
flashcards.back: Colors represent different frequency bands that can be assigned to base stations.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of the greedy graph coloring algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(V + E) where V is number of vertices and E is number of edges.
